turns.speaker: User
turns.text: They offered me 85k. I was hoping for 95k. I really want this job but I also know I'm underselling myself. How do I ask for more without seeming greedy?

turns.speaker: Assistant
turns.text: 'Greedy' is the wrong frame—you're negotiating based on market value. Try: 'I'm very excited about this role. Based on my research and experience, I was expecting a range closer to 92-95k. Is there flexibility there?'

turns.speaker: User
turns.text: That sounds much cleaner than what I had in mind. Should I mention competing offers even if I don't have any?

scored_turn.speaker: User
scored_turn.text: They offered me 85k. I was hoping for 95k. I really want this job but I also know I'm underselling myself. How do I ask for more without seeming greedy?

scores.rationale: Wants to assert but constrained by social fear of appearing greedy.
Self-awareness of undervaluing present, but confidence still shaky.
Clear hesitation to act on known self-interest.
